rationale: control_dependence: failure -- C02-P15 (execution errors/timeouts are returned to Aider to fix and re-attempt up to four times), C02-P33 (LaTeX compilation errors are piped back to Aider to correct), C02-P50 (an output-directory bug is automatically caught and fixed on every successful run). score -- C02-P09 (each idea carries '(self-assessed) numerical scores of interestingness, novelty, and feasibility'), C02-P10 (idea generation is prompted 'conditional on the existing archive, which can include the numerical review scores from completed previous ideas'). observation_result -- C02-P16 (Aider is given the experiment results and told to keep experimental-journal notes), C02-P19 ('Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment'). No fixed_or_predefined/not_reported: no unconditional autonomous progression is reported, and every step's dependence is stated. selection_signal names the concrete drivers: compile/runtime errors (C02-P15/P33/P50), the self-assessed idea scores (C02-P09), the automated review scores (C02-P36), the experiment results (C02-P16/P19), and literature similarity used to filter ideas (C02-P12/P13). selection_objective: local_repair -- the error-driven fixes (C02-P15/P33/P50). performance_improvement -- C02-P36 (automated review yields numerical scores for soundness, presentation, contribution, and overall quality, feeding the paper-evaluation loop). diversity_directed_selection -- C02-P12/C02-P13 name a post-generation SELECTION filter: 'After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API' and 'discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature'; this is a selection acting on the generated idea set to enforce novelty relative to prior work (reinforced by the novelty dimension in C02-P09), a pi-level diversity objective rather than a generation-stage diversification (contrast C03/C04/C06, where the diversity mechanism sits inside generation). The source frames it as pursuing not-already-published ideas, not as reducing posterior uncertainty, so no uncertainty_reduction or hypothesis_model_discrimination. generation_scope: fixed_space -- the experiment template/datasets are externally fixed (C02-P40 'we externally constrained the system to use these datasets, and in its current form, The AI Scientist cannot download higher-dimensional datasets'; C02-P02 plan-directed changes to a fixed experiment 'template'); no generation-side search-space-expansion or candidate-diversification mechanism is reported (the novelty pressure is exercised at selection, above, not in generation). candidate_selection_rule: threshold -- the explicit candidate down-selection is the novelty filter that discards ideas whose literature-similarity exceeds a 'too similar' cutoff (C02-P12/P13); the self-assessed and review scores (C02-P09/P10) condition idea GENERATION but the source states no separate top-k ranking that picks which ideas are executed. candidate_execution_rule: one_at_a_time -- C02-P14 ('plan a list of experiments to run and then executes them in order') and C02-P19 (the next experiment is planned and run sequentially, conditioned on the previous results); until_budget -- C02-P15 (re-attempt 'up to four times') and C02-P20 ('This process is repeated up to five times') bound the execution by a fixed try/round budget. No objective label appears in either rule. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- the numerical scores are self-assessed quality/review scores, not a Bayesian model-discrimination objective; no prior/posterior, likelihood, or EIG/epistemic-utility term is stated in any selection rule (corpus-level footnote).
